title: QA Tester
description:
We are looking for a detail-oriented QA Tester with 3 years of experience to ensure the quality and reliability of our applications. The ideal candidate will be responsible for testing web and/or mobile applications, identifying defects, and working closely with development teams to deliver high-quality products.

Key Responsibilities:Analyze requirements and create detailed test cases and test scenariosPerform manual testing of web, mobile, or desktop applicationsExecute functional, regression, integration, and system testingIdentify, document, and track bugs using defect management toolsCollaborate with developers and stakeholders to resolve issuesParticipate in Agile/Scrum ceremonies (daily stand-ups, sprint planning, retrospectives)Validate fixes and perform re-testingEnsure software meets quality standards and user expectationsContribute to test documentation and reportsRequired Skills:3 years of experience in Software Testing / Quality AssuranceStrong understanding of Software Testing Life Cycle (STLC) and SDLCHands-on experience in manual testingFamiliarity with bug tracking tools like JIRA, Bugzilla, or Azure DevOpsBasic knowledge of SQL for data validationUnderstanding of API testing (Postman or similar tools)Good knowledge of test case design techniquesStrong analytical and problem-solving skills

title: Jr. QA Tester
description:
Company Description

I.T. Excel is a new breed of service firm - an industry-focused consulting and integration firm offering a broad range of solution offerings to clients from a broad range of Industries. ITE is an end-to-end global consulting & solutions company with over 250 employee .We help companies bring improved focus to IT and how it aligns with the business, executes on its priorities and balances between innovation and efficiency. We have successfully shared with many of our clients the strategies, approaches and techniques necessary to transform IT capability and enable the implementation of business strategy. We specialize in the following technologies- QA/Software Testing, Business Analysis and Computer System Validation.



Job Description

RELOCATION IS A MUST AND 4 WEEKS MANDATORY IN-PERSON TRAINING IN RESTON, VA

Title: QA Analyst/ Jr. QA Tester/Business Analyst/BA/Jr. Validation Engineer

Qualifications: BS in Computer Science/ MS Computer Science/ BBA/ MBA/ BS in Biomedical Engineering/ BS in Chemical Engineering/ Engineering degree required from an accredited university or college. 
 
 Minimum Experience: Recent college graduates with no experience or graduates with few years of experience preferred. Computer literate and familiar with the use of word processing, spreadsheet, and basic database applications preferred.
 
 Employment Benefits:
 
 Competitive Base Salary: Base Pay of $55,000 - $58,000 / Yr with periodic reviews (usually every 6 months) and increments.

 I.T. EXCEL is an E-VERIFIED Company.

Clients: We mostly work with Fortune 50 companies. We have existing contracts/partnership with Amgen, Aventis, Alfa Physician Resources, Baxter, Bank of America, Bank One, Biogen, Cisco Systems, Citi Bank, Citizens Bank, Cymbal, Deutsche Bank, Eli Lilly, Freddie Mac, Fannie Mae, GMAC Insurance, IBM, ING Direct Bank, Johnson & Johnson, JP Morgan & Chase, LL Bean, Liberty Mutual, Nasdaq, Navimedix, Nextel, Pfizer, State Farm Insurance, Schering Plough, Wells Fargo, Verizon, 3 COM and many more.

 
 Health Insurance: ITE provides all its full-time employees with medical insurance. Health Insurance coverage (PPO Plan) is offered by Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield, one of the leading healthcare companies in the United States. This allows easy access to dependable healthcare, through a large network of physicians and hospitals, spread across the United States. We are also working on adding other benefits like Dental/Vision
 
 

Workers Compensation: Workers' compensation insurance is provided to all employees, covering them against work related injuries and/or disabilities. Relocation Assistance: We offer our employees Relocation Assistance, to help them with the relocation costs while starting their project at client site 

 



Qualifications

Qualifications: BS in Computer Science/ MS Computer Science/ BBA/ MBA/ BS in Biomedical Engineering/ BS in Chemical Engineering/ Engineering degree required from an accredited university or college. 
 
 Minimum Experience: Recent college graduates with no experience or graduates with few years of experience preferred. Computer literate and familiar with the use of word processing, spreadsheet, and basic database applications preferred.



Additional Information

Skills/Abilities/Competencies Required:
 
 Must be ambitious, have strong work ethic, and a willingness to learn
 Be a fast learner with strong problem solving skills
 Demonstrated strength in verbal and written communication
 Positive & winning attitude
 Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
 Ability to understand and effectively communicate at all management levels, technical dependencies, timeline impact and risks
 
 Employment Benefits:
 
 Competitive Base Salary.

title: Lead Quality Assurance Engineer
description:
Lead QA Automation Engineer (AI-Native & Security-Aware)

New York (Hybrid - 3 days on-site) | $150K–$185K Base | Full-Time




We're hiring on behalf of a well-funded, early-stage technology company in Midtown Manhattan. Small senior engineering team, high autonomy, real ownership. The platform operates in a stable, regulated industry with long-term demand.




This is not a maintenance role. You would own the entire QA function - reimagining automation through the lens of agentic AI and introducing security-aware testing practices as the platform scales. You'd report directly to the CTO and work alongside recently hired leads in backend engineering, data, and product.




What you'd be doing:

Own and evolve the QA automation framework for an AI-assisted development environmentBuild testing pipelines that work alongside AI-accelerated feature deliveryIntroduce security-aware testing into CI/CD - vulnerability scanning, AppSec automation, penetration testing conceptsPartner with backend and data engineering leads on release quality standardsBuild toward a model where QA and security validation scale with the team, not against it


What we're looking for:

7+ years in QA engineering with hands-on automation (Playwright, Cypress, Selenium, or equivalent)Actively using AI tools in your workflow - not just aware of them, but building a point of view on how they change testingGenuine familiarity with application security concepts - DAST/SAST, pen testing basics, vulnerability scanningYou've owned QA end-to-end in a fast-moving environment and set the standards, not just followed themStrong communicator who can operate autonomously in a lean team


Nice to have:

Experience in fintech or regulated-adjacent industriesExposure to agentic pipelines or LLM-integrated applicationsSecurity certifications (CEH, OSCP)Startup experience


JS/TS stack. Node backend. Equity, health insurance, 401K, flexible PTO.




If you want ownership, direct access to engineering leadership, and the chance to define what modern QA looks like in an agentic development environment.

description: QA Tester    HYBRID/ St. Louis, MO  Work is  HYBRID  w/Video interviews. 3+ years of experience in software testing mostly as a Manual Quality Assurance Test Engineer    Proficiency in testing webpages and related content pages on multiple types of devices.    Proficiency in writing test scripts and test cases for requirement validation, integration, regression, performance, and usability testing Ability to solve problems and troubleshoot quality assurance testing issues.  Ability to perform as a flexible, dependable self-starter capable of gathering information quickly and applying it to projects.    Demonstrated experience in an Agile work environment using Scrum methodologies and Agile tools (Digital.ai, formally VersionOne, or similar).

title: QA Tester
description:
QA Tester
Location: Coral Gables, Florida (Onsite)Employment Type: Contract
 
Role Overview
As a QA Tester, you will contribute to a variety of cross-functional projects aimed at driving significant improvements. Your projects will range in scope and complexity, necessitating expertise in diverse testing functions, including test development, execution, and project management. You will become a subject matter expert in your assigned workstream, enabling you to effectively support the operations and testing teams. Your efforts will ensure that we consistently deliver seamless experiences for our customers.
 
Key Responsibilities
Responsible for the application testing process for internal and external applications, which includes end-to-end user acceptance testing.Execute test scripts, report defects accurately and thoroughly, and retest efficiently.Collaborate with IT and other business units to solve real-time technical issues and conduct root cause analysis.Build a strong working understanding of the technical solution being delivered.
Required Qualifications
Experience: 3 years of relevant experience is required.
 
Preferred Qualifications
SAP knowledge is preferred.Exposure to Agile program development methodology.
This employer is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, or protected veteran status and will not be discriminated against on the basis of disability.
 
Apex uses a virtual recruiter as part of the application process. Click here for more details.
 
If you have visited our website in search of information on employment opportunities or to apply for a position, and you require an accommodation in using our website for a search or application, please contact our Benefits Department at leaverequest@apexsystems.com or 804-523-8228.

title: QA Tester
description:
Mandatory Qualifications: 
1.Level II- Between four (4) and seven (7) years of experience as a Tester 
2.Between four (4) and seven (7) years of experience: in quality assurance managing testing across multiple high-profile projects, with complex functional domains, within a matrix organization.
3.Between four (4) and seven (7) years of experience reporting and presenting to stakeholders and senior management. 
 
Desirable Qualifications: 
Four (4) to seven (7) years of work experience developing test plans, test cases, tracking defects and developing automated tests. 
2.Four (4) to seven (7) years of work experience leading and managing functional test planning and test execution activities. 
3.Four (4) to seven (7) years of work experience with automated testing using tools such as Selenium or Katalon Studio. Select...

6.Three (3) to five (5) years work experience creating reports and dashboards for test metrics using Jira or other tools. 
7.More than two (2) years of work experience in quality assurance of projects related to Child Support applications.

title: Oracle RMS QA Tester
description:
Job Title: Oracle RMS QA Tester

 Location: Blue Ash, OH 

  Essential Skills:
 Oracle RMS QE Tester with strong domain knowledge in Oracle Retail Merchandising System (RMS) to ensure quality, stability, and reliability of retail merchandising solutions.
 The role involves functional, integration, and regression testing across Oracle Retail modules, with close collaboration with business, development, and upstream/downstream system teams.
 
 Key Responsibilities:
 Perform functional and regression testing of Oracle Retail Merchandising System (RMS)
 Validate core merchandising business flows (items, purchasing, inventory, pricing, costing)
 Design and execute test cases based on business and functional requirements
 Conduct integration testing with upstream and downstream retail systems
 Perform data validation using SQL for transactions, inventory, and stock ledger
 Log, track, and manage defects through resolution and retesting
 Collaborate with developers, functional consultants, and business users
 Provide test status and quality metrics for releases
 
 Desirable Skills:
 Any experience with creating test data and utilizing test data management tools
 Any experience working in environments utilizing the OKR work process
 Any experience with automated testing in a cloud environment

title: QA Tester (Manual)
description:
About Peraton Peraton is a next-generation national security company that drives missions of consequence spanning the globe and extending to the farthest reaches of the galaxy. As the world's leading mission capability integrator and transformative enterprise IT provider, we deliver trusted, highly differentiated solutions and technologies to protect our nation and allies.


Peraton operates at the critical nexus between traditional and nontraditional threats across all domains: land, sea, space, air, and cyberspace. The company serves as a valued partner to essential government agencies and supports every branch of the U.S. armed forces. Each day, our employees solve the most daunting challenges that our customers face.


Visit peraton.com to learn how we're keeping people around the world safe and secure.


About The Role
Peraton is seeking a QA Tester (Manual). The Manuel QA Tester will perform the following duties, but not limited too:



Ensures that all required QA knowledge, artifacts and repositories are associated with VLER/SOES procedures
Test plans, test cases, and test data associated with the QA validation of high-quality software solutions DMDC's DVASP platforms.
Supports and maintains comprehensive manual test plans and verification protocols to validate software functionality, security compliance, and performance.
This includes executing manual functional, regression, integration, and accessibility testing, in alignment with DoD Section 508 requirements and Security Technical Implementation Guides (STIGs).
Devises testing plans, methods, and equipment to assure reliability of product in conjunction with product design and specifications.
Designs, develops, and implements testing methods and equipment.
Plans and arranges the labor, schedules, and equipment required for testing and evaluating standard and special devices.
Provides test area with parameters for sample testing and specifies tests to be performed. Compiles data and defines changes required in testing equipment, testing procedures, manufacturing processes, or new testing requirements.

Qualifications

5 years with BS/BA; 3 years with MS/MA; 0 years with PhD, 9 years with HS diploma
Must be a US Citizen
Must have active Public Trust clearance
Experience with manual testing tools
Experience performing manual tests
Knowledge of QA artifacts

Details
Target Salary Range: $66,000 - $106,000. This represents the typical salary range for this position. Salary is determined by various factors, including but not limited to, the scope and responsibilities of the position, the individual's experience, education, knowledge, skills, and competencies, as well as geographic location and business and contract considerations. Depending on the position, employees may be eligible for overtime, shift differential, and a discretionary bonus in addition to base pay.
Benefits Statement: Peraton offers eligible employees a variety of benefits including medical, dental, vision, life, health savings account, short/long term disability, EAP, parental leave, 401(k), paid time off (PTO) for vacation, and company paid holidays. A full listing of available benefits can be viewed at https://www.careers.peraton.com/benefits.
Application Duration Statement: The application period for the job is estimated to be 30 days from the job posting date. However, this timeline may be shortened or extended depending on business needs and the availability of qualified candidates.


EEO:
Equal opportunity employer, including disability and protected veterans, or other characteristics protected by law.
